channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Secret shopper study reveals telehealth prescribers rarely engage directly with weight-loss patients
item.description: A recent secret shopper study conducted by Yale researchers highlights significant gaps in clinical oversight for patients using weight-loss medications. The investigation, led by Ashwin K. Chetty—a m...

item.title: 2026 Emmys Nominations: The Pitt and Hacks Lead, The Bear Faces Downgrade
item.description: The 2026 Emmy Awards have unveiled their full nominations list, with The Pitt and Hacks emerging as the top contenders. The Pitt, which won the drama series category last year, leads with 25 nominatio...

item.title: Immigrant Chefs Preserve Cultural Heritage Through Cuisine
item.description: The article explores how immigrant chefs in cities like New Orleans, San Francisco, and New York are preserving their cultural identities through food. It challenges the 'melting pot' myth by highligh...

item.title: President Marcos Discusses Global Governance with Former UK Prime Minister Tony Blair
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos welcomed former UK Prime Minister Tony Blair during a meeting at Malacañang on July 8, 2026. The encounter focused on global governance and public policy, with Blair highlig...

item.title: Tanzania's Trade Fair Boosts Regional Economic Integration
item.description: The Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (Saba Saba) has evolved beyond a commercial exhibition into a strategic platform positioning Tanzania as a regional trade and manufacturing hub. Celebrating ...

item.title: Sultan Urges Northern Stakeholders to Unite Against Banditry and Terrorism
item.description: The Sultan of Sokoto, Alhaji Sa’ad Abubakar II, has called on all northern stakeholders to unite against the growing threats of banditry, terrorism, and kidnapping. Speaking at the 19th Northern Tradi...

item.title: Nelson Mandela's Capture Site: A Monument to His Arrest in Howick
item.description: The Nelson Mandela Capture Site, located near Howick in KwaZulu-Natal, marks the exact spot where Mandela was arrested on 5 August 1962, initiating his 27-year imprisonment. Sculptor Marco Cianfanelli...

item.title: ALSTOM and Tanzania Discuss Transport Infrastructure Investment
item.description: Tanzania’s Prime Minister Dr Mwigulu Nchemba has engaged in high-level discussions with French transport company ALSTOM to explore investment opportunities in the country’s transport infrastructure. T...

item.title: Former GOP Governor Criticizes Trump's Anti-Democrat Strategy as Misguided
item.description: Former Ohio Governor John Kasich mocked President Donald Trump's strategy of labeling Democrats as communists during an interview with MS NOW's Katy Tur. Kasich argued that Trump's attack on Democrats...

item.title: Margaret Qualley and Jack Antonoff Announce Separation After Three-Year Marriage
item.description: Margaret Qualley and Jack Antonoff are reportedly separating after five years of marriage, marking the end of a relationship that has influenced pop culture. The couple, married since 2023, were linke...

item.title: Former Girlfriend Sues Rep. Max Miller Over Settlement Violation
item.description: Congressman Max Miller (R-OH) faces legal action from his ex-girlfriend, Stephanie Grisham, who alleges he breached a non-disparagement agreement following her public claims of abuse. Grisham filed th...

item.title: Ayo Edebiri And Joy Sunday Get Emmy Nominations For Their Roles In 'The Bear' And 'DTF St. Louis'
item.description: Two Nigerian-American actresses, Ayo Edebiri and Joy Sunday, have bagged nominations for the 78th Primetime Emmy Awards. Edebiri, who plays Sydney Adamu in The Bear, is up for Outstanding Lead Actress...

item.title: Prince Edward Island's Green Party Surpasses PC Party in Provincial Polls
item.description: A recent poll by Narrative Research indicates that Prince Edward Island (PEI) could see the Green Party emerge as the leading force in the upcoming provincial election, potentially marking Canada's fi...

item.title: US Conducts Major Strikes on Iran in Response to Hormuz Attacks, Spurring Tensions and Oil Price Swings
item.description: The United States has launched over 80 strikes on Iranian military targets in retaliation for attacks on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions between the two nations. The strikes, ...

item.title: Cretin Family supergroup celebrates Ramones' 50th anniversary with tribute concert in LA
item.description: A new supergroup called Cretin Family, comprising members from Green Day, Blink-182, Rancid, and former Ramones bassist CJ Ramone, has been formed to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the Ramones' s...

item.title: Zverev edges Fritz in Wimbledon clash, Kostyuk advances to semis
item.description: Alexander Zverev secured his place in the Wimbledon semi-finals after a hard-fought victory against Taylor Fritz, ending a seven-match losing streak. The German second seed triumphed 6-4, 6-4, 6-2 on ...

item.title: Impeachment trial of VP Duterte enters third day with procedural developments
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Duterte entered its third day with procedural motions and legal arguments shaping the proceedings. Key developments included the filing of motions to dismiss ce...

item.title: Rugby Players Discuss Challenges of Intentional Knock-Ons and Match Preparations
item.description: Canan Moodie, a key Springboks player, highlights the immense pressure rugby referees face when judging intentional knock-ons during high-stakes matches. He notes that decisions must be made in millis...

item.title: Bijou Phillips Undergoes Successful Kidney Transplant After Health Crisis
item.description: Bijou Phillips, the actress known for her role in 'Raising Hope,' has shared an update on her health following a successful kidney transplant. The actor, who previously revealed her struggle with unde...

item.title: Trump's Mismanagement of Iran War Escalation Draws Criticism from National Security Expert
item.description: A national security scholar criticized President Donald Trump's handling of the Iran conflict, highlighting his inability to provide coherent responses to escalating tensions. Tom Nichols, writing for...
